what_it_does: Declares the cost/usage telemetry data model and operations for a UI contract: it specifies the per-turn ModelUsage shape, the OpenTelemetry metric set, the local stats-cache and /usage view, the org Usage & Cost API, and the headless budget-cap act, while recording which grains are built versus planned and how each must be read and rendered.
observations: A 'Refocus' note retracts previously cited company command-wrapper endpoints and says the capability is reached by driving a real Claude Code session; this entry remains a native data-model declaration.
Cost/usage has no identity of its own; it is telemetry attributed to a session/turn, model, skill/plugin/subagent, or org/workspace.

The per-turn grain is now built: as of 2026-06-12 the session supervisor stamps a `usage` block onto the durable `agent_sessions.turn` event, so per-turn spend can be read over the existing events stream.
Four machine shapes are defined: per-turn result message ModelUsage, OTel metric counters, on-disk stats-cache plus /usage view, and org Usage & Cost API plus Analytics.
`costUSD` in ModelUsage and local /usage figures are explicitly labeled as client-side estimates, not the authoritative bill.
The state model is stateless; the only adjacent stateful behavior is the headless `--max-budget-usd` cap, which lives on the session resource.
`cost-usage.get` is building for per-turn reads via events, planned for local `/usage` and the external Anthropic Usage & Cost API.
`cost-usage.act` is planned as a `cap-budget` spawn parameter that would pass `--max-budget-usd` through on session creation.
An INVENTORY note marks the Anthropic admin API as an external transport, not a company-owned endpoint.
standouts: The per-turn grain is explicitly built and e2e-verified by stamping usage onto `agent_sessions.turn`, while the other grains remain honestly labeled planned or external.
Strong epistemic guardrails: local cost figures are estimates and local usage is this-machine only.
The file contains a self-correcting 'Refocus' section and an 'INVENTORY note' that reject earlier phantom endpoint assumptions.
conventions: Embeds `contract:schema` and `contract:op` blocks for machine-readable shapes.
Cross-references other ledger entries with `[[...]]` wiki-style links.
Cites Atlas identifiers (e.g., CC-20.1, CC-20.2, CC-20.3).
Includes a reality table with local file size and build date.
Uses honest 'interim paths' and an inventory note to avoid claiming non-existent company routes.
concerns: The mix of built, planned, and interim paths across grains makes the overall status hard to read at a glance.
Some sentences are long and parenthetical, reducing scannability.
The planned `cap-budget` act is only a CLI-flag passthrough suggestion with no concrete company wiring described.
Date-specific claims (2026-06-12) may become stale quickly.
notes: A data-model contract for cost/usage telemetry that celebrates the newly-built per-turn event grain while honestly labeling local and org grains as planned or external.
questions: What exactly does the optional `model_usage?` subfield contain inside the stamped `usage` block?
How does a consumer aggregate per-turn usage blocks into a running total when no aggregation endpoint is provided?
What is the response schema for the Anthropic Usage & Cost API binding?
Is `--max-budget-usd` the only cost-control verb, or are other acts planned?
What does 'CC-20 adoption path' map to in the Atlas beyond the listed documentation sources?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to contract the shape of cost/usage telemetry a UI renders; the file does that and additionally tracks implementation status and epistemic limits, so it matches with extra operational honesty.
apparent_themes: Epistemic humility: estimates are not authoritative billing.
Event-driven observability: per-turn usage rides the events stream.
Honest status accounting: built, planned, and external surfaces are clearly separated.
intention_signals: To give the UI layer a single, truthful contract for rendering spend data while preventing the UI from misrepresenting client-side estimates as official bills, and to mark exactly which cost grains are actually available through the company fabric versus external or not-yet-built surfaces.
novelty: The file acts as both a schema contract and a build-status ledger, explicitly retracting earlier wrapper-endpoint claims and replacing them with a 'drive a real session' native-capture story.
contribution: A clear, grain-by-grain data model for cost/usage with explicit epistemic guardrails and a verified per-turn event binding that lets consumers read spend off the existing events stream without a new transport.
summary_for_embedding: A UI contract entry defining the cost/usage telemetry data model across per-turn result messages, OpenTelemetry metrics, local stats-cache, and org APIs, with built per-turn capture on agent_sessions.turn and planned local/org grains.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the UI layer with an honest, attributed cost/usage contract that distinguishes client-side estimates from authoritative billing and records which read surfaces are built versus planned.
